expectations.text: The skill correctly extracted business info from inline text (no files provided)
expectations.evidence: Cover note confirms: 'Extracted from inline text: sustainable residential architecture, biophilic design, natural materials, 50-year build horizon, tri-state audience.' All key brand fields populated correctly.

expectations.text: The blog post is between 500 and 700 words
expectations.evidence: Blog word count is ~561 words. Within the 500-700 range.

expectations.text: The blog post contains zero em dashes (—)
expectations.evidence: No em dashes found. All connective punctuation uses commas or periods.

expectations.text: The blog or social post topics relate to sustainable design, biophilic architecture, or building longevity
expectations.evidence: Blog covers biophilic design principles and the 50-year build philosophy. Instagram and LinkedIn posts are both about biophilic design. All pieces directly in scope.

expectations.text: The voice is thoughtful and considered — not punchy or trend-focused
expectations.evidence: Opening: 'Most people can recall a room they did not want to leave.' Long, considered sentences. No trend references. LinkedIn uses formal full sentences and academic framing ('perform measurably better for their occupants').

expectations.text: The carousel includes exactly 8 slides with visual concepts referencing forest green or warm sand tones
expectations.evidence: 8 slides present. Visual directions reference: 'Forest green palette' (Slide 1), 'Warm sand wall' (Slide 2), 'forest green through glass, warm sand stone floor' (Slide 1), forest green (#2D4A3E) and warm sand (#C4A882) throughout.

expectations.text: The reel concept is present with at least 6 scenes
expectations.evidence: 7 scenes with explicit time codes (0:00-0:08 through 0:51-0:58).

expectations.text: The LinkedIn post is distinct from the Instagram caption
expectations.evidence: LinkedIn is 4 paragraphs of professional analytical writing, opens differently ('The most well-designed rooms most people have been in did not feel designed.'), cites performance data, discusses specifications. Structurally and tonally distinct.

expectations.text: No generic design phrases like 'breathtaking', 'stunning', or 'transform your space'
expectations.evidence: None of these phrases appear. Language throughout is precise and functional: 'The untraceability is the point', 'built spaces perform measurably better'.
